title: Additional languages for the flagship guides
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. Future work: additional-language translations of the flagship guides, prioritizing the highest-need / lowest-take-up populations, each with per-language qualified bilingual review. The specific languages are TO BE SECURED (prioritized once the pilot jurisdiction's population is confirmed); this is a representative backlog task, not fanned out to one JSON per language.
objective: Translate the flagship guides into additional high-need languages, each with a qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off, prioritized by need / take-up gap.
acceptanceCriteria: Each additional-language translation requires a qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off (recorded in the per-language reviewer ledger); machine translation is draft-only
Each translation preserves citations, the not-advice framing, and the route-to-human pointers; benefits terminology and accuracy checked against the source guide
Languages prioritized by highest-need / lowest-take-up populations; specific languages remain TO BE SECURED (representative task, not fanned out until languages are confirmed)
